filter_logic: Halt trading when WVF > upper BB (22-period highest - low based)
expected_effect: Avoid capitulation days. Prevent MEDS-style tail risk.

filter_logic: Only enter when daily VIX < SMA(5)
expected_effect: Filter out rising-fear days. ~60% of trading days should pass.

filter_logic: Only enter fade trades when SPX is within VIX-implied range
expected_effect: Avoid entering when SPX already overextended beyond expected range.
notes: Use for both entry timing and strike selection.

filter_logic: Only trade credit spreads when VIX9D/VIX < 1.0 (contango)
expected_effect: Avoid backwardation regime where premium selling is dangerous.
notes: Highest priority filter. Directly addresses regime risk.

filter_logic: Halt all trading when VVIX > 140 or spikes 100%+ intraday
expected_effect: Emergency circuit breaker for crash scenarios.
notes: Would have prevented MEDS disaster on 2026-04-02.

filter_logic: Only enter when smoothed VIX < VIX MA (VIX in downtrend)
expected_effect: Ensure fear is declining before selling premium.
notes: Overlaps with VIX SMA(5) cross. Lower priority.

filter_logic: Use HMA direction on SPX 5min instead of EMA for trend determination
notes: Test as EMA replacement in trend-following strategies.

filter_logic: Skip Apex when VIX9D/VIX > 1.05 (backwardation). Conservative threshold to avoid killing too many of Apex's 100% WR trades.
expected_effect: Prevent the rare catastrophic loss that hasn't appeared yet in backtest. Insurance filter.
notes: Apex has 100% WR over 4.25yr. This is insurance against the unseen tail event.

filter_logic: Compute where SPX sits within VIX-implied daily range at 15:55. If >85% of range = skip call side. If <15% = skip put side.
expected_effect: Avoid selling credit spreads into an already-overextended move. Reduce the one-sided risk.
notes: Both-sides is Apex default. This smart-skips the dangerous side when price is at extreme.

filter_logic: Compute WVF on daily bar. If WVF > 2.0 std above mean at 15:50 skip entry. Catches multi-day selloffs that prior-day filter misses.
expected_effect: Catch intraday capitulation events. Complements prior-day return <1.5% filter.
notes: Prior-day filter catches yesterday's vol. WVF catches TODAY's panic.

filter_logic: Skip all LastFive trades when VIX9D/VIX > 1.0. Reduce sizing 50% when ratio > 0.95.
expected_effect: Reduce max drawdown from -$5.6k. Avoid regime where short premium is risky.
notes: LastFive already skips VIX>26. This adds structural regime awareness.

filter_logic: Skip PUT side when daily WVF > percentile(85). Market is in multi-day selloff - put spreads are dangerous.
expected_effect: Complement existing put momentum filter. Catches different pattern - slow grinding selloff vs. sharp afternoon drop.
notes: Two different put guards: momentum (intraday speed) vs WVF (multi-day panic level).

filter_logic: Replace fixed VIX buckets (d=0/3/5) with continuous: dist = ceil(1.5 * SPX*(VIX/15.87/100)*sqrt(5/390) / 5) * 5
expected_effect: More precise strike selection especially in VIX 18-22 zone where current buckets are coarse.
notes: Continuous calibration > discrete buckets. Should improve risk-adjusted returns.

filter_logic: Skip ALL trades when VVIX > 140 (absolute) or VVIX daily change > +30%.
expected_effect: Nuclear option - prevents trading during crash-like vol-of-vol. Very rare trigger (~5 days/year).
notes: Both strategies enter at 15:55 - last 5 min can still get crushed by late-day panic.
